src/flit/rendering/flex

  • Index

        hitTest
      • hitTest(r: RenderFlex; htResult: HitTestResult; position: Offset): bool

        performLayout
      • performLayout(r: RenderFlex)

Row/Column rendering. Two-pass flex layout matching Flutter's RenderFlex. Backs Row and Column; children are wrapped in RenderFlexChild with parent-data carrying their flex weight.

Imports

../foundation/render_object, ../foundation/geometry, ../foundation/color

Types

FlexFit = enum
  ffLoose, ffTight
How a flex child fills its allocated extent along the main axis. ffLoose: child may be smaller than its allocation (min = 0). ffTight: child must fill its allocation exactly. 
FlexParentData = ref object
  flex*: int
  fit*: FlexFit
  offset*: Offset
Parent-data attached to each flex child. Contributed by the Flexible / expanded proxy widget at attach time. 
RenderFlex = ref object of RenderObject
  direction*: Axis
  mainAxisAlignment*: MainAxisAlignment
  crossAxisAlignment*: CrossAxisAlignment
  mainAxisSize*: MainAxisSize
  textDirection*: TextDirection
  verticalDirection*: VerticalDirection
  children*: seq[RenderFlexChild]
The render object behind Row and Column. Lays children out along direction using mainAxisAlignment and crossAxisAlignment. mainAxisSize controls whether the flex shrinks to its children (msMin) or expands to its parent's max (msMax). 
RenderFlexChild = ref object
  obj*: RenderObject
  pd*: FlexParentData
A single child slot in a RenderFlex. Pairs the child render object with its parent-data.

Methods

method hitTest(r: RenderFlex; htResult: HitTestResult; position: Offset): bool {.
    ...raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Iterates children in order and recurses into the first one whose bounds contain the point. Adds itself to the path after the child returns so the path is ordered leaf-first. 
method paint(r: RenderFlex; ctx: PaintingContext; offset: Offset) {.
    ...raises: [Exception], tags: [RootEffect], forbids: [].}
Paints each child at the per-child offset computed during performLayout. Children paint in declaration order so the last child overlays the first (relevant only for overlapping children, which is rare in flex layouts). 
method performLayout(r: RenderFlex) {....raises: [Exception], tags: [RootEffect],
                                      forbids: [].}

Two-pass flex layout, mirroring Flutter's RenderFlex.

Pass 1: each child with flex == 0 gets loose constraints on the main axis (max = remaining space) and the configured cross constraints. Its size is recorded; the consumed main extent is subtracted from the remaining space.

Pass 2: each child with flex > 0 gets a main extent equal to (remaining * thisFlex / totalFlex). With fit = ffTight the extent is a tight constraint; with ffLoose the child may be smaller.

Finally the flex computes its own size (using msMax or msMin) and positions children along the main axis according to mainAxisAlignment.
